title: Content-based asset creation
desc: AI systems combine text-generation and text-to-image models to create visual content from text. They generate prompts, moderate content, and produce images for various applications.

images.alt: Figure 1: Content-based asset generation

title: Composable AI architecture
desc: The architecture diagram illustrates how AI applications can be built end-to-end on Cloudflare, or single services can be integrated with external infrastructure and services.

images.alt: Figure 1: Composable AI architecture

title: Multi-vendor AI observability and control
desc: By shifting features such as rate limiting, caching, and error handling to the proxy layer, organizations can apply unified configurations across services and inference service providers.

images.alt: Multi-vendor AI architecture

title: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
desc: RAG combines retrieval with generative models for better text. It uses external knowledge to create factual, relevant responses, improving coherence and accuracy in NLP tasks like chatbots.

images.alt: Figure 1: Knowledge seeding
Figure 2: Knowledge queries

title: AI Vibe Coding Platform
desc: Cloudflare's low-latency, fully serverless compute platform, Workers offers powerful capabilities to enable A/B testing using a server-side implementation.

title: Automatic captioning for video uploads
desc: By integrating automatic speech recognition technology into video platforms, content creators, publishers, and distributors can reach a broader audience, including individuals with hearing impairments or those who prefer to consume content in different languages.

images.alt: Figure 1:  Automatic captioning on upload

title: Ingesting BigQuery Data into Workers AI
desc: You can connect a Cloudflare Worker to get data from Google BigQuery and pass it to Workers AI, to run AI Models, powered by serverless GPUs.

title: Bot management
desc: Cloudflare has bot management capabilities to help identify and mitigate automated traffic to protect domains from bad bots.

images.alt: Figure 1: How Cloudflare identifies, scores and processes traffic from bots.

title: Designing a distributed web performance architecture
desc: A prescriptive pattern for building a Cloudflare-based L7 performance architecture that reduces latency, raises cache efficiency, and improves Core Web Vitals.

images.alt: Figure 1: Data flow overview
Figure 2: Smart Shield Advanced network diagram

title: Optimizing image delivery with Cloudflare image resizing and R2
desc: Learn how to get a scalable, high-performance solution to optimizing image delivery.

images.alt: Figure 1: Cloudflare Image Resizing and R2

title: Optimizing and securing connected transportation systems
desc: This diagram showcases Cloudflare components optimizing connected transportation systems. It illustrates how their technologies minimize latency, ensure reliability, and strengthen security for critical data flow.

title: Bring your own IP space to Cloudflare
desc: Cloudflare allows enterprises to bring their IP space to the Cloudflare network. This allows them to gain the security and performance of the platform while still appearing to the rest of the world via their own public IP space.

images.alt: Figure 1: Cloudflare announces customer IP range and proxies it to the origin server IP.
Figure 2: Protection against DDoS attacks can be placed in front of the BYOIP range in front of your Cloudflare tunneled network.

title: Optimizing device roaming experience with geolocated IPs
desc: Cloudflare can use private mobile networks (APNs) to connect devices roaming across multiple countries through regional Internet breakouts.

images.alt: Figure 1: Showing how Internet breakouts can present an egress IP that doesn't match the country the device is in.
Figure 2: Using Cloudflare you can ensure the egress IP as seen by Internet sites matches the country the device is roaming in.

title: Protect data center networks
desc: This document focuses on the reference architecture of using Cloudflare WAN, Cloudflare Network Firewall, and Cloudflare Gateway services.

images.alt: Figure 1. Protect Public-facing Networks from Inbound Traffic.
Figure 2. Protect outbound traffic from public-facing networks.
Figure 3.1. Protect inter-data center non-gateway-proxied traffic between private networks.
Figure 3.2. Protect inter-data center gateway-proxied traffic between private networks.
Figure 4. Protect outbound traffic from private networks.

title: Protect hybrid cloud networks with Cloudflare Magic Transit
desc: Cloudflare Magic Transit provides cloud-native, in-line DDoS protection, and traffic acceleration for all Internet-facing networks.

images.alt: Figure 1: Customer BYOIP for all Cloud and on-premises networks.
Figure 2: Customer lease IPs from Cloudflare for both on-premise and cloud network deployments.
Figure 3: Customer BYOIP for on-premise networks and lease IP from Cloudflare for cloud network deployments.

title: Protect public networks with Cloudflare
desc: This document explains how Cloudflare Magic Transit, Cloudflare Network Firewall, and Gateway work. The products offer in-line, automatic, scalable network protection for all Internet-facing networks. The architecture is designed to protect public networks across multiple clouds and on-premises.

images.alt: Figure 1: Protect the public networks from inbound traffic originating from the Internet.
Figure 2: Protect outbound Internet traffic originating from the public networks.

title: Protect ISP and telecommunications networks from DDoS attacks
desc: Learn how Internet service providers (ISPs) and telecommunications companies (such as T-Mobile or British Telecom) can protect themselves from DDoS attacks.

title: Extend ZTNA with external authorization and serverless computing
desc: Cloudflare's ZTNA enhances access policies using external API calls and Workers for robust security. It verifies user authentication and authorization, ensuring only legitimate access to protected resources.

images.alt: Figure 1: Showing a request to a private resource and where  Access can be customized for AuthZ and AuthN
Figure 2: Modified origin request including posture details

desc: Learn how to deploy Cloudflare One Appliance and evaluate your various deployment options.

title: Deploy self-hosted VoIP services for hybrid users
desc: Learn how Cloudflare improves over traditional VPN solutions by leveraging its global network.

images.alt: Figure 1: Cloudflare facilitates secure connectivity from user devices to the network where the SIP server is running.
Figure 2: For remote user to remote user, not all traffic flows over Cloudflare Mesh to the SIP server.
Figure 3: Remote user to on-premise user has all traffic routed via Cloudflare to SIP server and client.

title: DNS filtering solution for Internet service providers
desc: Learn how to use Cloudflare Gateway as a DNS filtering solution for Internet service providers.

title: Protective DNS for governments
desc: Learn how to use Cloudflare Gateway as a Protective DNS service for governments.

images.alt: Figure 1: DNS requests can be forwarded to Cloudflare via a variety of different methods.
Figure 2: Example DNS policy showing the use of a custom threat intel feed.
Figure 3: Showing how remote users can also redirect DNS requests for protection via Cloudflare.
Figure 4: An example of a DNS policy for users with the device agent. The policy uses group information from the identity provider so that it applies to a specific audience of users.
Figure 5: Show how lists can be used to provide custom hostname lists in the policy.
Figure 6: The different options available to use Cloudflare Gateway as a full-featured Secure Web Gateway.

title: Access to private apps without having to deploy client agents
desc: Learn how to provide access to private apps without having to deploy client agents.

images.alt: Figure 1: Remote browser connected to private web service using internal hostname

title: Secure access to SaaS applications with SASE
desc: Cloudflare's SASE platform offers the ability to bring a more Zero Trust orientated approach to securing SaaS applications. Centralized policies, based on device posture, identity attributes and granular network location can be applied across one or many Saas applications.

images.alt: Figure 1: Only traffic that has passed the Cloudflare network and relevant policies is authorized to access the SaaS application.

title: Zero Trust and Virtual Desktop Infrastructure
desc: This document provides a reference and guidance for using Cloudflare's Zero Trust services. It offers a vast improvement over remote access to web applications with greater security.

images.alt: Figure 1: Remote browser isolation can provide a secure, controlled browser environment for accessing sensitive company applications.
Figure 2: Two different traffic flow options: clientless RBI & RBI using the device agent.
Figure 3: Using Cloudflare Access ZTNA to secure VDI.
Figure 4: Using Cloudflare's Secure Web Gateway to filter and protect traffic coming from VDI.

title: Securing data at rest
desc: Learn how Cloudflare's API-driven Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) works and secures data at rest.

images.alt: Figure 1: Overall solution of user access controls to, and the discovery of, sensitive data.

title: Securing data in transit
desc: Data in transit is often considered vulnerable to interception or tampering during transmission. Data Loss Prevention (DLP) technologies can be used to inspect the contents of network traffic and block sensitive data from going to a risky destination.

images.alt: Figure 1: Securing data from the user device, all the way to the website/API
Figure 2: Various methods of connecting and routing traffic to Cloudflare to secure private traffic.
Figure 3: Example of a Cloudflare policy blocking confidential data uploaded to approved cloud storage.
Figure 4: Upload of file containing sensitive data blocked by Cloudflare DLP

title: Securing data in use
desc: Learn how Cloudflare's Remote Browser Isolation (RBI) works and secures data in use.

images.alt: Figure 1: Text copy/paste blocked by Cloudflare RBI.

title: Common user workflow for API Shield
desc: API Shield stages: endpoint discovery, review, positive security model (schema, mTLS, JWT), abuse protection, sensitive data detection, and endpoint monitoring — from Cloudflare Security Reference Architecture.

images.alt: API Shield has many stages: discovery, review, positive security model, abuse protection, data protection, and endpoint management/monitoring.
